Pricing
Request a demo
Get Notion free
Log in
What’s New
Keep up with the latest releases, improvements, and fixes.
Follow @NotionHQ
↗
July 1, 2026
3.6
Notion 3.6: External Agents, HTML blocks, and more
Your agents shouldn’t be stuck in one-off chats. Now you can bring multiple agents into a single UI with the rest of your team, so you can automate entire workflows end-to-end.
And there’s a lot more. AI Meeting Notes now include
speaker labels
(a top request!), your Notion Agents can read and write more filetypes like
Excel
and
PowerPoint
, and you can build
interactive HTML blocks,
like an ROI calculator.
Orchestrate External Agents (Claude, Cursor, and more soon)
Some of your agents might live in CLIs, IDEs, and separate apps, doing solo work the rest of the team can’t see or build on. Now you can assign them tasks from a board shared with your whole team, @-mention them like teammates, and watch them run.
Claude and Cursor are the first two External Agents in Notion
. That means you can reimagine entire end-to-end processes with full automation, right alongside the rest of your team’s work.
Get started →
Smarter meeting summaries with speaker labels
AI Meeting Notes now identifies speakers in your meetings
, based on whose microphone is active. This improves meeting summaries and makes sure follow-ups are assigned to the right teammate. And this is just the beginning; we’ll keep making speaker identification even better!
Download the desktop app for the best experience →
Add interactive HTML blocks to your Notion docs
Notion Agents can now create interactive
HTML blocks,
and we can’t stop tinkering with them. The best part is that they live in Notion, so anyone on your team can use them or iterate on them. Some examples we love:
Build a ROI calculator:
Watch your customers’ eyes light up when they see how much they’ll save by using your solution. Customize it with details like their industry or current tool stack.
Quiz your teammates:
Turn a doc about your latest feature into an interactive quiz your team will remember. Your Agent will identify what matters, create relevant questions, and score results at the end.
Map your org chart:
Visualize your people database as an org chart so new hires onboard faster and cross-functional work gets done. Do it directly in Notion, with no need to open another tool.
Try it out →
Your Notion Agents now speak Microsoft
Your Notion Agents now work with
PPTX, XLSX, DOCX,
and more. Give your agents
context
by attaching spreadsheets, decks, Word docs, and PDFs—your agent reads them like any other source.
And your agents can
create
these files for you too: turn a page into a polished PowerPoint, spin a database into an Excel model, or summarize a pile of PDFs into one clean doc. Your legal and finance teams will thank you. Notion 🤝 Microsoft.
Your agent can use Outlook for you
Another nice one for our Microsoft shops. Connect
Outlook Mail and Calendar
and your Notion Agents can run your inbox and your schedule for you. Triage incoming email, draft replies in your voice, and send messages; all you have to do is ask.
Your agent can find open times, schedule meetings, and keep your calendar in order so you spend less time wrangling logistics and more time on the work that matters.
Five new MCP connections for Custom Agents
People are loving Notion MCP—usage has 10×’d in the past month. Now your Custom Agents get five more connections out of the box:
Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, and ClickHouse
.
They join the connections already available (like Figma, GitHub, Zapier), so your agents can pull financial data from Mercury, dig into product analytics in Mixpanel, or update a Miro board, all with no custom setup required. Find and connect every available integration from the new connections tab in your settings.
Learn more →
More models, without the lock-in
Your agents support a fresh lineup of new models, so you can pick the right brain for the job, whether you need speed, deeper reasoning, or a creative spark. Use the latest frontier intelligence from
Opus 4.8
or
Grok 4.3
, or choose a more cost-efficient model like the new open-weight
GLM 5.2
.
Track Custom Agent actions in the audit log
Your security team now gets more visibility into how AI is being used. The
audit log
includes Custom Agent activity, so you can see when an agent runs, what it changed, and who triggered it, making it easier to review workflows, troubleshoot issues, and stay compliant. Available on Enterprise.
And a few more …
Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid
: Custom Agents now work across your full Slack org. Talk to your sales rep or
learn more
.
Notion Workers in the credit dashboard:
Find Worker usage in the Notion credits dashboard, so admins can track everything in one place.
Learn more →
Build Notion Workers on Windows:
Windows developers, you’re in. The Notion CLI now installs on Windows through npm, so building and deploying Notion Workers is no longer limited to Mac and Linux.
Read the docs →
Upload audio to AI Meeting Notes:
Bring meetings recorded elsewhere into Notion. Upload an audio file and AI Meeting Notes will transcribe and summarize it like any other meeting.
Duplicate custom agents:
Found a setup that works? Copy any custom agent in a click and tweak it for a new team or use case—no need to rebuild from scratch.
Agents can use your people directory:
Now you can give your agents access to your people directory, so they have more context about your team.
Learn more →
May 26, 2026
Merge cells in simple tables
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Finally! Merge cells in simple tables.
Create headers that span multiple columns or group related rows into structured layouts.
Give it a try: select multiple cells → open the cell menu → select
Merge cells
.
See the
announcement
on X →
May 13, 2026
3.5
3.5: Notion Developer Platform
Notion? For developers? Fair question. It’s true, we haven’t always been the most developer-focused platform. Today that changes.
Introducing: the
Notion Developer Platform.
Now you (and your coding agents) can write code to sync any data and build any agent tool, all running on our infrastructure. You can also bring your favorite agents into Notion (like Claude, Codex, Decagon, or ones you’ve built yourself). It turns Notion into one shared canvas with all your data, for your team and agents to work together.
Learn more →
Orchestrate all your team’s agents (Alpha)
Bring your favorite agents into Notion with the External Agents API, even the ones you built yourself. We’ve also partnered with Claude, Codex, Decagon, and more so they work out of the box. Now Notion is your orchestration layer: a Decagon ticket routes to your coding agent, which proposes a fix and loops in your team to approve. Anyone can work with agents in Notion, not just engineers.
Join the External Agents waitlist →
Notion is our AI layer because it’s where work is created or imagined—and we want our agents as close to the action as possible.
Dan Gilbert
CEO at Brainlabs
Sync
any
data source (Beta)
Sync any data source with an API into your Notion databases. No servers for your team to manage. Our new database sync is powered by Workers that run on our infrastructure (more on this below). Pull in tickets from Zendesk so agents can take a first pass on the fix. Sync customer data from Salesforce for agents to build detailed reports. Connect Strava and Spotify data to curate the perfect running playlist. Whatever context you need can now live in Notion.
Watch the demo →
Workers give us the tools to build deep integrations into Notion that simply couldn't exist before. We have a worker that runs every night that syncs and converts uneditable PDFs in our Google Drive into rich, fully editable pages in an organized Notion database. It unlocks this data for our team and agents - and saves us tons of time.
Sam Lambert
CEO at PlanetScale
Build custom tools for your agents (Beta)
Give your Custom Agents capabilities that Notion and MCP don’t cover on their own. Write your logic in code and deploy it as a Worker. It’s
deterministic
, so it’s more reliable than LLM reasoning, and a
fraction of the token cost
. Use them to generate assets, query internal data, or take action in any other app.
Read the docs →
I think of Notion Workers as infrastructure: they auto-populate, auto-update, and set up the systems I need.
Austin Tedesco
Head of Growth at Every
Trigger Notion workflows from anywhere (Beta)
Webhooks used to be a one-way street: Notion could trigger your other apps, but not the other way around. Now any app can trigger Notion directly. A Worker receives the webhook, runs your logic, and takes action in Notion or calls other APIs. Use it to close tasks when a PR merges, update your CRM when a subscription changes, or create an onboarding doc when an offer is signed.
Read the docs →
Meet your Notion Workers
Database sync, agent tools, and webhook triggers are all powered by a new primitive we’re calling Workers. Notion Workers are our hosted runtime for custom code, so you can extend Notion without running your own servers. You and your coding agent write the code, deploy it through the CLI, and run it in a secure sandbox. Workers are free to try during the beta period. Starting August 11 2026, Workers will run on Notion credits.
Read the docs →
Workers let us connect directly to other tools’ APIs and automate what used to be manual handoffs. Notion becomes the connective layer, and Workers fill in whatever gaps exist between your tools.
Brian Emerick
Technical Program Manager at Vercel
A Notion CLI, built for devs and coding agents
The Notion command-line interface (CLI), made specifically for developers and coding agents, is a new way to work with Notion programmatically. Use it to sign in to your workspace, read and take action in Notion, build and deploy Workers, and extend Notion however your team needs. To install, run curl -fsSL https://ntn.dev | bash.
Watch the demo →
Use your Notion Agents in any app (Alpha)
Soon, your Notion Agents won’t have to stay in Notion. With the Notion Agent SDK, you can embed an agent inside your other tools. Trigger a deal report from a button in your CRM. Answer repeat questions inside MS Teams or Discord with verified knowledge from your workspace. Or pull customer context into Amplitude, Hex, or any dashboard.
Join the Agent SDK waitlist →
Manage all connections from one tab
We’ve updated the Connections tab in workspace settings. Now, every connection lives in one place, so your team can see everything that’s available at a glance. It includes personal and workspace connections, personal access tokens for API authentication, and internal API connections. And each app shows every connection type in one listing. Go to
Settings
→
Connections
to check it out (or
click here
).
Agents “hall of fame”
Knowing what agents to build can be the hardest part, so we pulled together the best agents from companies like Ramp, Clay, and Vercel into one library. Each one comes with a checklist of exactly what you need (databases, pages, tools) and a starter prompt to copy/paste. Pick one and set it up in minutes.
Browse the collection →
More updates your devs will love
Markdown API:
ICYMI read and write Notion pages as Markdown. Built for the way agents already think.
Notion MCP:
Now works with Meeting Notes and block comments, plus creating and updating databases are 91% more token-efficient.
Notion API:
Any member can build connections (not just Workspace Owners). Plus workspace-scoped OAuth and personal access tokens.
See releases →
Developer Portal:
app.notion.com/developers
is now a dedicated portal for creating, managing, and listing your connections and tokens.
Developer Docs:
Rebuilt and streamlined for clarity with a built-in AI assistant to help you find what you need.
This is just the beginning for the
Notion Developer Platform
. Any data, any tool, any agent, all running on our infrastructure. We can’t wait to see what you build.
Keep the feedback coming!
Ivan
P.S. We announced all of this and more at Make with Notion: Developer Platform.
Watch the keynote →
P.P.S. Curious what teams are already building? See how
Every
,
Brainlabs
, and
Vercel
are using our developer platform in production.
P.P.P.S. One more thing. You can now merge cells in simple tables, just like a spreadsheet. We’re excited about this one too.
May 7, 2026
Plan Mode
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
When you ask your agent to make significant changes like rewriting pages or updating databases in bulk, results can drift off course if your intent is unclear. Plan Mode introduces a preliminary step where your agent asks clarifying questions and builds a detailed plan before acting. The result is fewer surprises and more confidence in complex, multi-step work.
Learn more about
Plan Mode
from the Help Center →
May 6, 2026
New Custom Agent Directory
Play
Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing.
Please watch it on
YouTube
Custom Agents now have a dedicated place in your Library. Use the Custom Agent Directory to browse all your workspace’s agents, pin your favorites, and create new ones to automate your team’s busywork 24/7.
Get started from the sidebar:
Library
→
Agents
.
May 5, 2026

hits.title: Docs – Open source alternative to Notion or Outline
Notion – All-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, and databases
Focalboard – a self-hosted alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana

Lessons learned from sharding Postgres at Notion

analysis.name: Notion
analysis.tagline: The AI workspace that works for you.
analysis.value_proposition: An AI-native workspace that unifies docs, knowledge base, projects, and autonomous agents so teams can capture context, find answers, and automate work end-to-end.
analysis.ideal_customer: Cross-functional teams (eng, product, design, marketing, IT) at startups through Fortune 100 that want a single system of record combined with AI agents that act on it

features.name: docs
features.description: Rich collaborative documents built from composable blocks
features.evidence_quote: Docs. Simple and powerful.

features.name: knowledge base
features.description: Central source of truth for teams and agents
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Base. One source of truth for teams and agents.

features.name: projects
features.description: Project tracking with less overhead, more progress
features.evidence_quote: Projects. Less tracking. More progress.

features.name: databases
features.description: Organize projects/tasks with subtasks, dependencies, custom properties
features.evidence_quote: Databases including subtasks, dependencies, custom properties and more

features.name: database views
features.description: Multiple visualizations (table, board, calendar, timeline) of database data
features.evidence_quote: Database views

features.name: sub-tasks and dependencies
features.description: Hierarchical task relations inside databases
features.evidence_quote: subtasks, dependencies, custom properties

features.name: charts
features.description: Visualize database data as donut, bar, and line charts
features.evidence_quote: Visualize data from databases in different ways (e.g. donut, bar, and line charts). Unlimited charts

features.name: forms
features.description: Basic and custom forms to capture responses into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Basic forms ... Custom forms ... Remove Notion branding from public forms

features.name: sites
features.description: Publish Notion pages as websites; custom themes, favicon, GA
features.evidence_quote: Publish a Notion page to the web ... Custom sites ... integrate with Google Analytics

features.name: web clipper
features.description: Browser extension to save web content into Notion
features.evidence_quote: Web Clipper

features.name: notion calendar
features.description: Standalone calendar app that connects multiple calendars
features.evidence_quote: Connect multiple Calendars to manage work and time together. Notion Calendar

features.name: notion mail
features.description: Email client integrated with Notion workspace
features.evidence_quote: Notion Mail

features.name: notion ai
features.description: Generative AI to write, summarize, and autofill databases
features.evidence_quote: Trial AI capabilities like generating docs, or autofilling databases

features.name: notion agent
features.description: Autonomous agent that completes multi-step tasks using workspace context
features.evidence_quote: Does work for you. Completes complex, multi-step tasks using context from Notion, your connected apps, and the web. Notion Agent

features.name: custom agents
features.description: User-built AI agents that automate repetitive team work
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents. Automate repetitive work for your team.

features.name: external agents
features.description: Bring Claude, Cursor and other outside agents into Notion workflows
features.evidence_quote: Claude and Cursor are the first two External Agents in Notion

features.name: q&a agents
features.description: Agents that answer questions from existing workspace knowledge
features.evidence_quote: Q&A agents. Answers questions instantly using knowledge you already have.

features.name: task routing agents
features.description: Agents that assign, prioritize, and route tasks automatically
features.evidence_quote: Task routing agents. Assigns, prioritizes, and routes tasks on its own.

features.name: reporting agents
features.description: Agents that summarize, write, and send recurring reports
features.evidence_quote: Reporting agents. Summarizes, writes, and sends reports for you.

features.name: ai meeting notes
features.description: Automated meeting transcription, summary, and follow-ups
features.evidence_quote: Automate your meeting notes and follow-ups, no bot needed. AI Meeting Notes

features.name: speaker labels
features.description: AI Meeting Notes identifies speakers by active microphone
features.evidence_quote: AI Meeting Notes now identifies speakers in your meetings

features.name: audio upload for meeting notes
features.description: Upload recorded audio to be transcribed and summarized
features.evidence_quote: Upload an audio file and AI Meeting Notes will transcribe and summarize it

features.name: enterprise search
features.description: Unified search across Notion and connected apps like Slack and GitHub
features.evidence_quote: Search across connected apps like Slack, Github & more. Enterprise Search

features.name: citations in answers
features.description: AI answers include citations back to source pages
features.evidence_quote: Every answer, instantly—with citations.

features.name: html blocks
features.description: Interactive HTML blocks (calculators, quizzes, org charts) generated by agents
features.evidence_quote: you can build interactive HTML blocks, like an ROI calculator.

features.name: verified pages
features.description: Mark pages up-to-date with a verified badge shown in search/AI citations
features.evidence_quote: Add a verified badge to pages that are up to date. Appears in search results and AI citations.

features.name: granular database permissions
features.description: Row-level access so collaborators only see assigned records
features.evidence_quote: Limit access to rows where the collaborator is assigned.

features.name: private teamspaces
features.description: Teamspaces hidden from non-members for sensitive work
features.evidence_quote: Create teamspaces that can't be seen or discovered by anyone except those you add.

features.name: guests vs members
features.description: Distinct guest role for external collaborators
features.evidence_quote: Members vs. guests

features.name: sharing and permissions
features.description: Page/workspace-level sharing controls
features.evidence_quote: Sharing & permissions

features.name: comments and reminders
features.description: Inline comments and time-based reminders for collaboration
features.evidence_quote: Collaborate with others using comments and reminders.

features.name: page history
features.description: Restore pages to previous versions
features.evidence_quote: Restore your page to a previous version. Page history 7 days ... Unlimited

features.name: import and export
features.description: Bring data in and take it out of Notion
features.evidence_quote: Import & export your data

features.name: automations
features.description: Trigger workflows on database changes
features.evidence_quote: Automations

features.name: connections
features.description: Native integrations with Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, and more
features.evidence_quote: Connect to some tools like Slack & Google Drive. Basic connections

features.name: premium connections
features.description: Higher-tier connections including GitHub and Asana
features.evidence_quote: Connect to GitHub, Asana & more. Premium connections

features.name: advanced connections
features.description: Enterprise-tier expanded integration catalog
features.evidence_quote: Connect to even more tools. Advanced connections

features.name: mcp connections
features.description: Model Context Protocol connectors for Custom Agents (Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, ClickHouse, Figma, GitHub, Zapier)
features.evidence_quote: Five new MCP connections for Custom Agents ... Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, and ClickHouse

features.name: notion workers
features.description: Custom code to build agent tools, sync external data, and trigger workflows
features.evidence_quote: Extend Notion with custom code to build agent tools, sync external data, and trigger Notion workflows

features.name: notion cli
features.description: Command-line tool to build and deploy Notion Workers (Mac, Linux, Windows via npm)
features.evidence_quote: The Notion CLI now installs on Windows through npm

features.name: public api
features.description: Developer API for building integrations
features.evidence_quote: Public API

features.name: saml sso
features.description: Enterprise single sign-on via SAML
features.evidence_quote: Manage employee access at scale with secure single sign-on. SAML SSO

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: User and group provisioning via SCIM API
features.evidence_quote: Access the Notion SCIM API to provision and manage users and groups. User provisioning (SCIM)

features.name: domain verification
features.description: Verify email domain ownership to unlock advanced security
features.evidence_quote: Verify ownership of an email domain to access advanced security features

features.name: domain management
features.description: Claim, transfer, or delete workspaces on a verified domain
features.evidence_quote: Establish ownership of your company domain and take actions on workspaces

features.name: audit log
features.description: Detailed log of security and workspace activity, including Custom Agent actions
features.evidence_quote: Access a detailed log of security and safety-related activity

features.name: advanced security and controls
features.description: Prevent external sharing, disable guests, workspace-level rules
features.evidence_quote: Unlock extra permission controls to prevent certain people from sharing pages externally

features.name: zero data retention
features.description: LLM providers apply zero data retention for Enterprise workspaces
features.evidence_quote: our LLM providers utilize zero data retention for Enterprise plan workspaces

features.name: dlp and siem connections
features.description: Integrate with SIEM and DLP tools for compliance
features.evidence_quote: Security & Compliance connections (DLP, SIEM)

features.name: customer success manager
features.description: Dedicated CSM for Enterprise accounts
features.evidence_quote: Customer success manager

features.name: notion academy
features.description: Learning platform with badges and certifications
features.evidence_quote: learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications

features.name: templates marketplace
features.description: Large gallery of user- and Notion-made templates
features.evidence_quote: Life 33401 templates ... Work 24112 templates

features.name: consultants marketplace
features.description: Directory of certified Notion consultants
features.evidence_quote: Consultants

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps
features.evidence_quote: iOS & Android

features.name: desktop apps
features.description: Native Mac and Windows clients
features.evidence_quote: Mac & Windows

features.name: multi-model support
features.description: Choose across frontier and open-weight models (Opus 4.8, Grok 4.3, GLM 5.2)
features.evidence_quote: Use the latest frontier intelligence from Opus 4.8 or Grok 4.3, or choose ... open-weight GLM 5.2

features.name: custom agents for slack enterprise grid
features.description: Custom Agents run across a full Slack org
features.evidence_quote: Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid

features.name: people directory
features.description: Agents can query the org's people directory
features.evidence_quote: Agents can use your people directory

features.name: microsoft file support
features.description: Agents read/write PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, PDFs
features.evidence_quote: Your Notion Agents now work with PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, and more.

features.name: outlook integration
features.description: Agents triage Outlook Mail and schedule via Outlook Calendar
features.evidence_quote: Connect Outlook Mail and Calendar and your Notion Agents can run your inbox

differentiators: AI-native workspace combining docs, KB, projects with agents that act on the same data
External Agents: Claude and Cursor can be assigned tasks and @-mentioned like teammates
Interactive HTML blocks generated by agents (ROI calculators, quizzes, org charts)
Model choice across frontier and open-weight LLMs without lock-in
G2's #1 knowledge base for 3 consecutive years; 62% of Fortune 100 and 98% of Forbes Cloud 100 use Notion
Verified pages that flow into AI citations for trust
MCP-based extensibility with rapidly growing connector catalog

recent_ships.what: Notion 3.6: External Agents (Claude, Cursor), interactive HTML blocks, speaker labels in AI Meeting Notes
recent_ships.source: Notion 3.6: External Agents, HTML blocks, and more
Your Notion Agents now speak Microsoft

recent_ships.what: Outlook Mail and Calendar connection for agents
recent_ships.source: Your agent can use Outlook for you

recent_ships.what: Five new MCP connections: Mercury, Mixpanel, Miro, Box, ClickHouse
recent_ships.source: Five new MCP connections for Custom Agents

recent_ships.what: Model support for Opus 4.8, Grok 4.3, GLM 5.2
Custom Agent activity in the audit log (Enterprise)
recent_ships.source: Track Custom Agent actions in the audit log

recent_ships.what: Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid
recent_ships.source: Custom Agents for Slack Enterprise Grid

recent_ships.what: Notion CLI now installs on Windows via npm
Upload audio files to AI Meeting Notes for transcription
recent_ships.source: Upload audio to AI Meeting Notes

recent_ships.what: Notion Workers beta — custom code extending Notion
recent_ships.source: Extend Notion with custom code ... Starts using credits on August 11

praised: 'Notion's thoughtful design speeds up collaboration and decisions' — Renee Solorzano
'Using the most AI-native tools like Notion is an important competitive advantage' — Michael Truell, Cursor
G2's #1 knowledge base for 3 consecutive years
HN interest in Notion as an all-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, wikis, databases
complaints: HN: 'Notion is withholding my company data, what can I do?' — data lock-in / export friction concerns
HN thread traction for open-source alternatives (Docs, Focalboard, Confluence/Notion clones) indicates desire for self-hostable substitutes
G2 review content was not accessible during scrape (page returned no content)

free_tier.limits: Unlimited pages for individuals but limited pages/blocks for 2+ members, 5MB file upload cap, 7-day page history, trial of Notion AI

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Trial of Notion AI, basic forms/sites, Notion Calendar, databases w/ subtasks & dependencies

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited blocks/file uploads, custom forms/sites, unlimited charts, basic connections (Slack, Google Drive)

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Notion Agent, AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search (beta), SAML SSO, granular DB perms, private teamspaces, premium connections

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: SCIM, audit log, advanced security, DLP/SIEM connections, zero data retention, CSM, domain management

pricing.notes: Custom Agents free to try, then $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits. Notion Workers free to try, start using credits on August 11. Yearly billing offers up to 20% off. Some plans (Plus, Enterprise) marked as 'may not be available for every workspace'.

recent_hn.title: Notion shutting down its AI-powered email client, including Mac and iOS apps
Why do teams keep losing context, and why hasn't any tool fixed it?
Show HN: Valmis, an OpenClaw alternative built for work, with security in mind
Show HN: I built an automated productivity system (Telegram –> Notion)
Show HN: Morning Report – daily briefing compiled from Sentry, Linear, Gmail,etc
